name: bioethics_near_miss_event_generates_stonehaven_car_fair
premise: A hospital wants to use leftover embryos for training; religiously affiliated partners threaten to withdraw funding. In Bioethics, the situation tagged internally around bioethics near miss event generates stonehaven car fair has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A union or patient advocate group is on the line demanding a written commitment before the next operational shift. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Regulators or auditors are en route with a ninety-minute ETA; the first documented response will anchor every later question. No option cleanly satisfies both care and fairness; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Talia Dubois
agents.identity: I am Talia Dubois, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Bioethics. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat fairness as disposable collateral every time care spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Talia Serrano
agents.identity: I am Talia Serrano, the counsel or integrity officer watching downstream liability in Bioethics. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so care cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes fairness in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will impose a narrow, time-boxed intervention with human override, subgroup checks, and a hard stop if harm rises—accepting slower relief to protect fairness.
I will authorize a broad immediate action to stabilize outcomes now, with post-hoc measurement and correction—accepting elevated risk to fairness to secure care before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Talia Dubois must choose under care versus fairness. What does Talia Dubois do?
